captions.text: The limits of same-weights disagreement are real. Pitfalls, later.

lines.text: The fix is called the GroupChat or debate pattern. Instead of one model answering, you run a structured conversation between multiple agents. Each plays a distinct role. They challenge and refine each other before converging on an answer.

Usually that’s the same model called several times with different system prompts. And before you object: yes, same weights. Is the disagreement real? More than you’d expect. A critic doesn’t need independent knowledge. It needs a different objective. A system prompt provides exactly that. We’ll get to the limits of this later, because they’re real too.

There’s research behind this. Multi-agent debate measurably improves factuality and reasoning. Follow-up studies are more mixed, and the gains concentrate in reasoning-heavy tasks. Keep that in mind, it’ll matter at the end.
